fields.description:
## Context
Umbrella for the 2026-06-09 audit, workstream 3: architecture recommendations balancing reuse with simplicity, optimizing for reliability and maintainability by LLM agents (units small enough to edit effectively without fragmenting the codebase). Full analysis: session-logs/2026-06-09-architecture-review.md (local, gitignored). Verdict: the event-sourcing/concurrency core (I1-I9) is sound — protected asset, no redesign. The liabilities are: no CI running the 1,400+ tests, four god-units, ~140 bash-embedded heredocs, dual read plumbing, and reconciler write/read seam bypasses. Includes the regression-safe bash->Python strangler-fig strategy and flags which changes must land inside the post-announcement breaking-change window.

## Success Criteria
- CI gate (coily-conch-tag) lands FIRST; every subsequent child rides a green pipeline.
- In-window items (rich-glare-sake, fare-rant-clasp, fatty-cipher-range) land before real consumer adoption.
- No module in src/ over the 800 LOC soft cap once the split/migration children complete.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All children closed or explicitly deferred with a recorded decision
- [ ] Breaking-window items shipped inside the window (or the miss documented + a deprecation path defined)
- [ ] Architecture review re-run after Phase 1 to confirm risk list shrinkage
